name: nyyon-figures
description: Render editorial diagrams and featured covers from a spec — locally, no network, no model. Neutral default theme; fully re-themeable (colors, fonts, brand).
long_description: A local, offline renderer for clean editorial diagrams. Ships 16 parametric diagram templates (contrast, layers, cycle, fanout, columns, grid, funnel, timeline, quadrant, pyramid, venn, table, pipeline, radial, bigstat, progression) plus a 1200x630 featured cover, themeable global settings (paper/ink with a single accent — colors, fonts, wordmark and URL all overridable at startup, in code, or at runtime via set_theme; the default is a neutral slate/blue + IBM Plex look, not any specific brand), an article→figures flow, ad-hoc one-off rendering, and the reasoning prompt that maps an article to a figure set. The calling assistant decides which templates/slots to use, then calls the render tools — which rasterize SVG to PNG at 2x via resvg and write the files locally. Nothing leaves the machine.

tools.name: figures_for_article
tools.description: START HERE: article in → a brief for the chosen design (auto / all / cover / a template), then render.

tools.name: list_templates
tools.description: List the 16 diagram templates + cover with their slot schemas.

tools.name: get_settings
tools.description: Get the active theme (colors, fonts, sizes, brand).

tools.name: set_theme
tools.description: Adjust the global design — colors/fonts/brand — for all later renders.

tools.name: render_figure
tools.description: Render one diagram from a template + slots to a PNG.

tools.name: render_cover
tools.description: Render the 1200x630 featured cover from title/kicker/highlight/sub.

tools.name: render_set
tools.description: Render a whole figure set + cover in one call.
